title: solanum luteum subsp. alatum
intro: Solanum luteum subsp. alatum, also known as the golden daisy bush or alate yellow nightshade, is a flowering plant native to Australia and Tasmania, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive winged sepals. This plant is used in traditional herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Solanum luteum subsp. alatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
